questions.question: Why is assumption logging valuable in a system design interview?
options: It replaces the need for clarification questions entirely.
It lets you move forward under ambiguity while keeping your reasoning explicit and revisable.
It guarantees every assumption will be correct.
It is mainly useful for frontend design prompts.
questions.explanation: The value is preserving momentum without hiding uncertainty.

questions.question: Which assumption is most worth recording explicitly?
options: An assumption that materially changes topology, consistency, or capacity choices.
An assumption about whether the interviewer likes bullet points.
An assumption about the IDE theme used during implementation.
An assumption that has no effect on architecture either way.
questions.explanation: Design-relevant assumptions deserve airtime because they affect the answer.

questions.question: What is the strongest way to state an assumption?
options: Hide it inside the diagram and hope the interviewer infers it.
State it clearly, tie it to a design decision, and note what changes if it flips.
Mention it only at the end if there is time.
Use as many assumptions as possible to avoid making commitments.
questions.explanation: An assumption is most useful when it informs and bounds the design.

questions.question: Which failure is most dangerous when assumptions are not logged?
options: The quiz becomes harder to grade.
Different parts of the answer drift into contradictory worldviews without being noticed.
The candidate uses too few service names.
The architecture becomes too simple to discuss.
questions.explanation: Silent contradictions are especially costly in long interview answers.

questions.question: Why is the 'flip impact' of an assumption important?
options: It shows how the design must adapt if reality differs from the current assumption.
It guarantees the interviewer will reveal the hidden requirements.
It removes the need for a trade-off section.
It proves the initial assumption was definitely wrong.
questions.explanation: Good assumption handling anticipates redesign, not just the current branch.

questions.question: What trade-off comes with keeping the assumption log deliberately short?
options: It stays usable in real time, but some low-value ambiguity will remain undocumented.
It always reduces design correctness.
It prevents any operational reasoning later.
It only works when the system is single-region.
questions.explanation: Brevity makes the tool practical even though it is not exhaustive.

questions.question: Which metric best reflects healthy assumption discipline?
options: The number of assumptions that are explicitly tied to concrete design consequences.
The total number of assumptions regardless of usefulness.
The number of databases mentioned per assumption.
Whether every assumption is fully validated before design begins.
questions.explanation: Quality comes from relevance and linkage, not raw count.

questions.question: If the interviewer corrects one of your assumptions midway through the answer, what should you do?
options: Ignore the correction so the narrative stays consistent.
Update the assumption, restate the changed branch, and explain what design consequences follow.
Restart from the beginning without summarizing the delta.
Defend the original assumption to show confidence.
questions.explanation: The goal is adaptability with clear reasoning, not stubbornness.
